originalPrompt: Framework: Express, Port: 7995, Styling: Tailwind CSS

subtasks.title: Initialize project and base Express config
subtasks.description: Set up a Node.js project and basic Express server. Steps: npm init -y; install express and nodemon; add scripts { start: "node server.js", dev: "nodemon server.js" }; create server.js to listen on port 7995; enable express.json(); serve static files from ./public; verify GET /health returns 200.
subtasks.successCriteria: Running npm run dev starts Express on http://localhost:7995 and /health returns 200; public/ is served.

subtasks.title: Implement flashcard model and JSON storage
subtasks.description: Create a simple Flashcard model { id, front, back }. Implement ./lib/storage.js using fs to read and write ./data/cards.json atomically. Provide functions: getAllCards(), addCard({front,back}), updateCard(id,{front,back}), deleteCard(id). Ensure validation for non-empty strings.
subtasks.successCriteria: data/cards.json is created on first write; storage functions perform CRUD and reject invalid payloads with clear errors.

subtasks.title: Build Express CRUD API endpoints
subtasks.description: Expose REST endpoints: GET /api/cards, POST /api/cards, PUT /api/cards/:id, DELETE /api/cards/:id. Use storage functions, return appropriate HTTP codes (201 on create, 200 on update/delete, 400 on validation errors, 404 when id not found). Add basic error handler middleware.
subtasks.successCriteria: All endpoints respond correctly with JSON; CRUD operations persist to cards.json; invalid data and missing ids are handled with proper status codes.

subtasks.title: Add sample seed data on server boot
subtasks.description: On server start, check if cards.json is missing or empty. If empty, insert 2–3 sample flashcards (e.g., basic Q/A). Implement as a small async init routine called before app.listen. Ensure seeding runs only once and does not duplicate.
subtasks.successCriteria: First run creates sample cards; subsequent runs do not duplicate; GET /api/cards returns samples when file was empty.

subtasks.title: Set up frontend scaffold and Tailwind CSS
subtasks.description: Create public/index.html and public/app.js. Include Tailwind via Play CDN in index.html. Define a minimal layout container. Constrain UI to only red, black, and white using Tailwind utility classes (text-black, bg-white, border-black, text-red-600, bg-red-600).
subtasks.successCriteria: Opening http://localhost:7995 serves index.html with Tailwind styles applied; only red, black, and white colors are used.

subtasks.title: Implement minimal flashcard UI
subtasks.description: Build a clean interface: header, list of cards, and an add/edit form. Show each card with front/back text and Edit/Delete buttons. Use a simple modal or inline form for editing. Apply minimal Tailwind styles with red accents and black/white surfaces.
subtasks.successCriteria: UI displays existing cards, provides inputs for front/back, and shows clearly labeled Edit and Delete controls with minimal styling.

subtasks.title: Wire frontend to Express API for CRUD
subtasks.description: In public/app.js, implement fetch calls to the API: load cards on page load, submit new cards via POST, update via PUT, and delete via DELETE. Render updates without full reload, handle errors gracefully, and disable buttons during requests.
subtasks.successCriteria: Users can add, edit, and delete cards from the UI and changes persist; network failures show simple error messages; no page reload is required.

subtasks.title: Test flows and document run instructions
subtasks.description: Manually test end-to-end on port 7995: load, create, edit, delete, and refresh to confirm persistence. Verify only red, black, and white are used. Add a brief README with setup, scripts, and curl examples for API usage.
subtasks.successCriteria: All CRUD flows work reliably; app runs via npm run dev on http://localhost:7995; README includes clear steps and example API calls.

decompositionStrategy: Backend-first Express API with JSON-file storage, serving a minimal static frontend using Tailwind (Play CDN). Incremental layering: storage -> API -> frontend -> wiring -> test.
